top_categories.workout_context: 64ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Etape Caledonia. Train it until it feels boring.
At mile 60 of 80, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Etape Caledonia's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
At Etape Caledonia, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Etape Caledonia's paved Scottish Highland roads with sustained forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 80 miles.
5,100ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 80 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,100ft of climbing on paved Scottish Highland roads with sustained does to your power file on race day.
5,100ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 80 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Tempo is Etape Caledonia's race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 80 miles on paved Scottish Highland roads with sustained. Train it until it's automatic.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Etape Caledonia’s 80 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on TT Threshold, G Spot, and Durability to prepare you for 80 miles of paved scottish highland roads with sustained rolling climbs in Pitlochry, Perthshire, Scotland, UK.
